earned_content: Readiness labels bound: ready-for-agent, in-flight, staging, ready-for-human, needs-info; no label = ungroomed (groom target, never run target)
Do NOT substitute 'ready for planning' / 'ready for implementation' for ready-for-agent — those are the pre-existing HUMAN workflow stage labels and stay neutral

in-flight is set by run at dispatch replacing ready-for-agent; records branch name + dispatch date in a comment; cleared on abort, replaced by 'staging' on merge to staging, or reset only via groom's human-confirmed orphan sweep
'staging' label = merged into staging branch but issue stays open pending staging→main promotion; native issue closure supersedes it when promotion lands on main
in-review has no label on GitHub — an open PR expresses it

Work-type labels: bug, enhancement (plan-then-implement unless small), refactor, research (dossier under research/<slug>/), draft (judgment-terminal, human review verdict is done, artifact kept; label provisioned 2026-07-10)
Routing rule: terminal question about sources → research; sources feeding voice/persuasion prose → draft; behavior changes keep code work-type with research as substage

Dispatch metadata required before ready-for-agent: Surface (backend/ui/mixed/non-code + capability), Coordination class (routine | orchestrator-required), Coordination reason — in a stable 'Dispatch:' block; missing metadata → run skips, never infers

Exclusion labels: wontfix, duplicate, invalid, superseded; neutral: documentation, good first issue, help wanted, question, ready for planning, ready for implementation, implementation in progress, planning in progress, blocker
blocker label is priority metadata, NOT a dependency edge

Dependencies: GitHub native issue dependencies (commands in platform.md) + legacy body lines 'Blocked by #123'/'Depends on #123' both checked; read the dependency LIST not the summary counters (counters can lag)
Label aliases: none — role labels map 1:1
ready-for-agent applied only to issues the human confirms in the shortlist; agent may self-apply ready-for-human/needs-info/exclusions on clear evidence
Concurrent runners possible; in-flight is an optimistic claim marker — run re-reads each issue immediately before marking and skips changed ones
Orphan sweep: in-flight issue whose recorded branch is gone or quiet >7 days is a corpse — groom surfaces to human for reset; NEVER silently reset (branch may hold unmerged work)
Base branch is STAGING for issue work and feature PRs; main is production; fork from origin/staging remote-tracking ref without checking out through a dirty primary checkout
Branch naming: codex/<issue-number>-<slug>

PR gives GitHub CI lint/format/typecheck; Vercel previews may exist but no workflow guarantees one; squash-merge into staging; push to staging deploys PartyServer staging, push to main deploys PartyServer production
pnpm dev starts: Next web app, server-side client, PartyKit, React Email preview, Postgres, QStash dev server, Redis, serverless Redis HTTP
URLs via portless: http://app.metis.localhost, http://party.metis.localhost, http://emails.metis.localhost
Fixed ports: Postgres 5302, Redis 6379, QStash 8081, serverless Redis HTTP 8079, server-side client 3001, PartyKit 1999
Worktree regime: cloud-singleton for full app verification — stack depends on shared Clerk tenant, Google Drive service account, Upstash/ngrok, PostHog, fixed portless hostnames

pnpm setup:worktree (scripts/setup_worktree.sh) copies .env* files, installs deps, runs pnpm check + pnpm build — but does NOT remap ports, compose projects, container names, hostnames, datastore URLs, or cloud creds

Full 16-row shared-singleton collision table: metis-postgres container name, port 5302, default Compose project + postgres_data volume, copied DATABASE_URL, ports 8081/6379/8079/3001/1999, portless hosts, Clerk tenant, Google Drive account, AgentMail inbox, PostHog project, hosted QStash/Upstash/ngrok/Resend, staging PartyKit/Railway/Vercel — each marked locally-isolatable or not
Worktree audit 2026-07-14: setup manual, no remapping; treat a worktree unready until its env files (incl apps/cli/.env) are present
Seed regime: none — drive the app; CLI can load JSONL snapshots: pnpm -C apps/cli dev:cli -- load cases|persons|tasks <path>

Auth for testing: sign in via browser as AgentMail inbox in repo global .env (AGENT_EMAIL), trigger Clerk email code/magic link, read code via AGENTMAIL_API_KEY; same inbox serves OTP and invitation flows; never hardcode/print secrets

Per-issue fixture naming: VERIFY-<issue>-<purpose>; provision before criteria loop, retain through final evidence, issue coordinator removes only its own fixtures; never share mutable scratch entities across issues
Scale-sensitive criteria are BLOCKED until a plan records the real limit or an approved synthetic substitute

Drivers: shell + pnpm -C apps/cli dev:cli for CLI; no Playwright/Cypress found; browser via machine agent-browser with isolated profile; ChatGPT-in-Chrome only with per-use explicit consent; Computer Use gated on recorded use case + explicit approval
Independent runtime verification: delegate to codex exec with self-contained prompt for real UI interaction/screenshots/second opinion

Deferred (unverified) checks: Clerk login, AgentMail retrieval, browser driving, screenshots, recording — do not claim UI verification until effect observed; ffmpeg and screencapture installed but screen-recording permission unverified
Review surface: tailnet, Funnel off, effect-verified 2026-07-14; root https://ashers-macbook-pro.tail045dd5.ts.net/review
Run tailscale status before publishing; tailscale up only because a review is being published; on failure use local fallback; never Funnel or a public tunnel
LaunchAgent com.asher.backlog-surface serves ~/.backlog/surface on loopback port 8377; persistent proxy: tailscale serve --bg --set-path /review http://localhost:8377
Static publish: ln -sfn <absolute-file> ~/.backlog/surface/metis/<issue>/<name>.html

Interactive review: python3 .agents/skills/review-loop/scripts/review-server.py --doc <file> --title --issue --kind --state <state-dir> --surface ~/.backlog/surface/metis --port 0 --public-url https://ashers-macbook-pro.tail045dd5.ts.net/metis/<issue>/review --hub-url .../review/metis/ --surface-label metis; then tailscale serve --bg --set-path /metis/<issue>/review http://localhost:<port>
Await verdict in Floor-class watcher: review-await.py --state <state-dir> --timeout <s>; exit 124 re-arms; 0/3/10 deliver verdict
Stop/reap: review-server.py --stop --state <dir>; tailscale serve --set-path /metis/<issue>/review off; hub sweep: review-server.py --sweep --surface ~/.backlog/surface/metis
Live prototype path: tailscale serve --bg --set-path /metis/<issue>/proto http://localhost:<port>
Keep-awake: none — surface available only while the Mac is awake
Staffing: no project floor/ranking/provider/succession override; metis adds only worktree isolation for parallel Claude→Codex edits
Parallelism verdict: serialize-verification — worktrees may implement in parallel but only ONE thread may bring up or exercise the full stack at a time; no exception lane
Tools: authenticated gh CLI; agent-browser; pnpm (commands from .intent/config.json and package.json); Codex subagents for delegated issue/review loops
Tracker binding: github dunnharland/metis via authenticated gh CLI

Blocker read: gh api repos/dunnharland/metis/issues/<n>/dependencies/blocked_by (list, effect-verified 2026-07-14); use list not .issue_dependencies_summary counters (they lag); also parse legacy 'Blocked by #N'/'Depends on #N' body lines

Blocker write: blocker_id=$(gh api repos/dunnharland/metis/issues/<blocker> --jq .id); gh api --method POST repos/dunnharland/metis/issues/<blocked>/dependencies/blocked_by -F issue_id="$blocker_id"; DELETE .../blocked_by/$blocker_id to remove — effect-verified on throwaway issues 2026-07-14

Close-on-merge: feature PRs target staging so they reference issues WITHOUT closing; run thread applies staging label after merge; the staging→main promotion PR must list Closes #<n> for every staged issue
PR: gh pr create --base staging --title <t> --body-file <path> — ready-for-review, never draft; approval signal is an exact 'LGTM' comment; a HUMAN merges (squash into staging), the loop never merges
Worktree creation: git worktree add <path> -b codex/<issue>-<slug> origin/staging; pnpm setup:worktree for env/deps but no remapping
Pinned-SHA semantics: squash-merge rewrites history — plans/evidence pinned to pre-merge branch SHAs are orphaned after merge; re-pin to the merged staging commit (or branch-name ref for evidence)

Harness: Claude Code native Agent thread with isolation: worktree; Codex native agent thread naming the worktree; cross-harness worker routes: codex exec --cd <worktree> -m gpt-5.6-sol '<prompt>' from Claude; claude -p --model opus '<prompt>' </dev/null (no --bare) from Codex — worker routes only, never coordinator-eligible
Spawned threads read bundled references at .agents/skills/backlog/reference/ (Claude via .claude/skills/backlog symlink)
Durable wakeup: Claude Code Monitor mechanism; Codex dedicated native watcher thread; no webhook wired

Test frameworks: vitest in @metis/webapp (pnpm --filter @metis/webapp test); Node tsx --test in server-side packages via their package test scripts; root aggregate pnpm test; pass a file path to run a single test file
Tests colocated with source (foo.test.ts / foo.test.tsx beside the module)

Commit style: Conventional Commits; PRE-PUSH HOOK may auto-commit lint/format fixes as 'chore: auto-fix lint and format issues' (hook lives in .git/hooks/pre-push, runs pnpm lint:fix + format:fix and commits)
No sign-off requirement

Conventions: prefer pnpm commands, snake_case filenames for NEW files, respect existing package/module boundaries, defer to AGENTS.md + docs/ + linters; UI work follows docs/UI.md and verifies responsive behavior in browser
(rest is the shipped tdd-derived test-first doctrine — template, replaceable)

Check commands (prefer .intent/config.json): pnpm --filter @metis/webapp test -- <path>; pnpm --filter @metis/server-side-client test; pnpm --filter @metis/yjs-persistence test; pnpm test; pnpm format; pnpm lint; pnpm lint:ws; pnpm typecheck; pnpm build (effect-verified 2026-07-14 pass)
Aggregate pre-PR gate: pnpm check = format+lint+typecheck+test+workspace lint; effect-verified 2026-07-14 with one existing non-failing lint warning and one workspace-lint warning as baseline
CI workflow .github/workflows/ci.yml jobs: lint, format, typecheck, build-boot — runs on every PR branch and merge queue
CI does NOT run test suites — pnpm test is local-gate-only, green CI does not prove tests pass; build-boot builds production bundles and boots server-side client (local gate does not)
CI-green is procedural, not host-enforced: protection/rulesets API returned 403 on 2026-07-14 (private repo lacks required GitHub plan) — human merger must wait for the four checks manually
Acceptance criteria from the issue + (enhancements) the approved plan's definition of done, written as pass/fail checks against a running app

Repo-wide expectations every change must satisfy: Clerk auth boundaries, Yjs document persistence, migration behavior, Google Drive integrations, background job side effects, responsive/accessibility for UI

Evidence bars: logic/backend = green targeted tests + broader check output; UI = desktop AND mobile width screenshots/recordings + a11y notes; data/migration = before/after data evidence or focused persistence test
Second-opinion verification delegates to codex exec when a criterion needs real UI interaction/screenshots
Evidence = real check output + artifacts driven through the actual app; probes/dry-run transcripts are NOT evidence in this repo (runnable surface exists)
Committed under evidence/<slug>/, never MP4; files named c<criterion>-<what-it-shows>.png
GIF recipe: ffmpeg -i in.mp4 -filter_complex "fps=12,scale=960:-1:flags=lanczos,split[a][b];[a]palettegen[p];[b][p]paletteuse" out.gif — ≤~10s, well under 10 MB

GitHub inline-render rule: use github.com blob URLs with ?raw=1 (not camo-proxied, work on private repos); raw.githubusercontent.com and /raw/<sha>/ 404 on private repos; no API path to the drag-drop CDN
Embed form: [![<criterion>](https://github.com/<owner>/<repo>/blob/<sha>/evidence/<slug>/<file>.png?raw=1)](same URL without ?raw=1)
Mechanical verification (agent can't see the render): gh api repos/<o>/<r>/commits/<sha> (404 = unpushed/orphaned); git cat-file -e <sha>:evidence/<slug>/<file>; extension PNG/JPEG/GIF never MP4
Squash-merge orphans pinned SHAs — re-pin or use branch-name ref, never 'fix' by switching to plain links
Styling-only capture reuse only when HEAD is still the final reviewed HEAD and Reviewer records exactly 'no product-code change; no recapture'; any drift forces fresh final-HEAD evidence
Captured once after adversarial review converges, each artifact mapped to the criterion it proves; evidence step posts nothing itself — hands the block back
Plan threshold: more than one subsystem/public interface, OR new data models/migrations/external deps, OR risky/hard to reverse/> ~1 day
Plans are self-contained HTML committed at plans/<issue-number>-<slug>.html; inline styles + pre-rendered inline SVG (mmdc for Mermaid), no rendering libraries, no external fetches
Stable anchors: acceptance criteria are <li id="ac-N" data-criterion> rows; review annotations and verify/evidence key on these ids
Approval = hash-bound approve event on the review-loop surface; thread/tracker comments are NOT approval
On request-changes: ledger disposition for every annotation — changed/kept/orphaned — before re-awaiting
Approved plan committed to the WORK branch before implementation dispatch
Posterity: after approval, comment a compact markdown digest (headings, ACs, Mermaid) on the issue plus repo link to the HTML

Repo-specific scrutiny list: Clerk auth boundaries, Yjs persistence and migrations, Google Drive/service-account access, background jobs and email side effects, API compatibility, accessibility, responsive UI, accidental use of staging/production credentials in local/test flows

Presumptive blockers: file growing past ~1000 lines (ask decomposition first), ad-hoc conditionals/one-off flags in unrelated flows, feature logic leaking into shared paths, thin wrappers/any-churn, avoidably sequential orchestration
Open every test/fixture/document cited as criterion coverage and confirm it exercises the claimed runtime seam
Product-semantics questions are ruling requests to the issue coordinator — stop and wait, never invent behavior
Approval is exactly the comment 'LGTM'; styling-only evidence reuse verdict is exactly 'no product-code change; no recapture' only when HEAD is still final
(smell baseline + ambition section are the shipped Fowler/Cursor template)
PR title = Conventional Commits subject, because squash-merge into staging makes the PR title the commit subject

Body order: Closes-linkage + work-type, Summary (incl scope discovery), Changes (design reasoning not file list), Plan (SHA-pinned link), Checks run (each command from verifying.md + result), CI status (disclosed red/pending, never omitted), Verification (per-criterion outcomes + caveats, environment gaps vs product issues), Evidence placeholder 'Captured after review converges' (research work-type: Research dossier section instead)
Reviewer's structural pushes are in scope by default; address every actionable comment unless it changes product behavior beyond issue scope
Reply to each addressed comment with what changed + commit SHA; intentional non-fix gets reason + risk; re-prompt Reviewer once the iteration is handled
Seams: Vitest in @metis/webapp, tsx --test in server-side packages; one web regression: pnpm --filter @metis/webapp test -- <path>; aggregate: pnpm test
Log inspection commands: pnpm --filter @metis/server-side-client logs:workflows; pnpm --filter @metis/server-side-client logs:redis; pnpm --filter @metis/webapp logs:db
pnpm dev for full-stack reproduction; no flaky surfaces recorded; no house-practice substitutions
Register prototype scripts in the owning package's package.json (web: apps/web, @metis/webapp) — pnpm scripts + Turbo
Routing: Next.js app in apps/web; UI conventions per docs/UI.md; snake_case filenames for new files
UI variants gated by ?variant= URL param hosted in the real page; screenshot each variant via the environment.md driver and embed in the plan
Test commands: vitest via pnpm --filter @metis/webapp test; tsx --test in server-side packages; root pnpm test
Repo cares about: snake_case filenames, monorepo package boundaries, existing store and migration patterns, TypeScript/linting/style docs under docs/
Durable research root: research/<slug>/; scratch shards in system temp, not committed; research stays out of evidence/ unless it proves a change criterion
Primary local sources: repo code, git history, tests, migrations, docs/; external: official provider docs/APIs via authenticated connectors or browser sessions
Cite stable file/line, commit, URL, page, or record locators; preserve customer data and credentials
Serialize shared database or provider mutations; present findings as repo-local Markdown/HTML via global Presentation policy

earned_content: Work domain: software — step playbooks scaffolded from templates/software/; recorded 2026-07-11 (issue #32)
Readiness label bindings are ALL identity mappings: ready-for-agent, in-flight, ready-for-human, needs-info, needs-shaping — no aliases; setup reused existing labels
in-flight records what's flying via a GitHub comment alongside the label (branch name and dispatch date)
GitHub expresses in-review/closed natively — open PR is in-review, closure via 'Closes #<n>' at merge; no extra labels

Work-type labels (identity): bug → diagnose branch; enhancement → implement branch; refactor → refactor branch; research → source-audit branch (dossier kept under research/<slug>/); draft → produce-and-review branch, judgment-terminal, done at human review verdict, no mechanical verify pass/fail, artifact is KEPT (vs prototype = throwaway)
Routing rule: terminal question about sources → research; sources feeding voice/persuasion prose → draft; behavior change → code work-type with research as substage
Exclusion labels: wontfix, duplicate, superseded, invalid; neutral: documentation, question, good first issue, help wanted

Dispatch metadata block required on every ready-for-agent issue: surface (backend/ui/mixed/non-code + capability), coordination (routine/orchestrator-required), reason, and route: direct for enhancements; missing fields are a grooming gap, never permission to infer
Dependencies: GitHub native blocked_by relation (verbs in platform.md); duplicate/supersede = 'duplicate of #N'/'superseded by #N' body line plus exclusion label
Readiness: agent applies ready-for-agent only to human-confirmed shortlist; may apply ready-for-human/needs-info/needs-shaping/exclusions on its own
Orphan sweep: in-flight issue whose recorded branch is gone or quiet past 7 days is a corpse — groom surfaces to human as candidate reset; never silently reset (branch may hold unmerged work)
Concurrency: in-flight is an optimistic claim marker; run re-reads each issue immediately before marking and skips any that changed
Base branch main; branch naming <issue-number>-<slug> (e.g. 6-slim-backlog-composer)
No CI, no preview deploy, no deploy on merge — skills consumed via npx skills add; merge just updates main
This is a skills repository, not a running product: no dev stack, no services, no ports; 'running the app' = driving a probe scenario through an executor
Worktree regime: local-isolatable — git worktree add <path> -b <branch> main is the complete isolated stack
Shared singletons: GitHub tracker (serialized main-branch writes) and tailnet presentation surface (one review published at a time); neither forces serialized code verification
Seed regime: none — the state a skill needs is a probe scenario (prompt + answer key) in the skill's evals/
Auth: gh CLI authed as asasher (keyring); Codex CLI authed to its own subscription (codex --version → 0.144.1), billed separately; credentials in gh keyring and ~/.codex/, never hardcode or echo

Verification fixtures: VERIFY-<issue>-<purpose> under temp dir or owning <skill>-workspace/; never share a mutable fixture across issue runs; synthetic prompt fixtures OK for skill decisions but not for live GitHub API behavior or script lifecycle checks
Drivers: in-session Claude subagent reading SKILL.md (primary); independent executor codex exec -s read-only --skip-git-repo-check (or -s workspace-write); stdlib scripts driven directly with python3
Evidence per surface: eval transcript + pass/fail verdict table against answer key; screenshots for visual artifacts; terminal transcripts for scripts

Presenting: tailnet surface, Funnel off, private to MacBook + iphone-14-pro-max; ALWAYS open published URL locally with open <url> — standing instruction from Asher, every presentation, no need to ask
Root URL https://ashers-macbook-pro.tail045dd5.ts.net/asher-skills (tailscale 1.98.8); surface dir ~/.backlog/surface/asher-skills holding registry.json + generated index.html + per-issue docs

Tailnet bring-up: check tailscale status first (non-zero + 'Logged out.'/'Tailscale is stopped.' when down); run tailscale up only when down AND publishing now; never down/up to reset a healthy connection; auth URL/failure → surface to Asher, fall back to open <file>, never Funnel or public tunnel

Document server: sandboxed macOS tailscale app cannot serve file paths — python3 -m http.server 8390 --directory ~/.backlog/surface/asher-skills then tailscale serve --bg --set-path /asher-skills http://localhost:8390; port 8377 is already held by a prior /review handler
Publish a doc: mkdir -p ~/.backlog/surface/asher-skills/<issue> && ln -sfn <abs path> ~/.backlog/surface/asher-skills/<issue>/<name>.html

Review server (self-host path): python3 skills/software-development/serve-via-tailnet/scripts/review-server.py --doc --title … --issue --kind plan --state --surface ~/.backlog/surface/asher-skills --port

--public-url //review; proxy via tailscale serve --bg --set-path /asher-skills//review http://localhost:
Verdict wait: python3 …/review-await.py --state <dir> --timeout <secs> — exit 0 approve / 3 nits / 10 changes / 124 timeout
Hub sweep: review-server.py --sweep --surface ~/.backlog/surface/asher-skills

Reap rule: remove issue symlinks and tailscale serve --set-path /asher-skills/<n>/review off (and /proto off); tailscale serve status lists live handlers; known stale pre-existing handler /review → http://localhost:8377 — reap if dead (tailscale serve --set-path /review off)
Keep-awake: none (setup choice) — no LaunchAgent, no caffeinate; AFK reviews outlasting a run may find the machine asleep
Staffing deltas: skill design is orchestration-grade; probe evals use the dual-executor contract; no project floor/capability-provider/succession override
Parallelism verdict: serialize-verification — a genuine POLICY choice (user chose sequential at setup), not a hard constraint; flip to parallel-safe by re-running backlog setup
Tracker binding: github, repo asasher/asher-skills, gh CLI authed as asasher, https protocol

Verified verbs: gh issue list --state open --json number,title,labels,body; gh issue view <n> --comments; gh issue comment; gh issue edit --add-label/--remove-label; gh issue create --label <work-type>,<readiness>; gh issue close
Read blockers: gh api repos/asasher/asher-skills/issues/<n> --jq '.issue_dependencies_summary' — .blocked_by > 0 defers the issue (verified against native dependency summary)

Write a blocker link: resolve numeric id via gh api repos/asasher/asher-skills/issues/<blocker> --jq '.id', then gh api -X POST repos/asasher/asher-skills/issues/<blocked>/dependencies/blocked_by -F issue_id=<numeric-id> — verified live; do not substitute task-list prose
Close-on-merge: 'Closes #<n>' in PR body is the loop's default; direct gh issue close only for issues no PR carries

PR verbs: gh pr create --title … --body-file; gh pr edit --body-file; gh pr view --comments; inline threads via gh api repos/asasher/asher-skills/pulls/<n>/comments; approval = exact 'LGTM' comment; merge = human on GitHub or explicitly authorized merge-changes (gh pr merge <n> --squash --delete-branch) — the automated loop never merges
Version control: worktrees optional under sequential verdict; sync base via git fetch origin && git checkout main && git pull --ff-only; publish via git push -u origin <branch>
Pinned-SHA semantics: force-pushes after evidence capture orphan pinned blob URLs — re-pin per evidence.md

Harness: Claude Code; native spawn via Agent tool (isolation: 'worktree' when needed); Claude→Codex via bounded codex exec --cd <dir> through its tracked wrapper; Codex→Claude via bounded claude -p --model <model> '<prompt>' </dev/null and NEVER --bare
Reachability is directional: a failed Codex→Claude invocation removes only that route; Claude→Codex remains; no vendor-policy or credit monitor gates dispatch
Spawned threads read skill references under .claude/skills/<name>/ and docs/agents/ in the checkout
Wakeups: ScheduleWakeup/Monitor for polling; verdict waits block on serve-via-tailnet's review-await.py; longer watches via watch-until ladder — a watcher subagent, never the orchestrator inline
Skill prose is the product; no history, no unnecessary negations — a prohibition earns its place only against demonstrated drift (CONTEXT.md redundant-negation rule)
Catalog gate: PATH=/usr/bin:$PATH python3 tools/test_catalog.py (17 tests)
Site manifest gate: PATH=/usr/bin:$PATH python3 site/check.py — its exit code is the verdict; don't pipe through tail in a && chain or the failure is masked

Staffing eval suites run from skills/system/staffing/evals/: test_global_apply.py, test_global_templates.py, test_provider_pilot.py; compiled per-provider staffing load has a hard byte budget (BASELINE_BYTES * 0.8 in test_global_apply.py) — prose added to install-and-reconcile.md or harness.md must fit inside it
Runner trap: bare == in a zsh command line (e.g. echo ===) breaks the whole command — quote or avoid
Probe answer keys cite exact sentences from the skill's files — editing a cited sentence means re-citing the key in the same change
Python tests are stdlib unittest, one file per tool, run directly
Mounts are build products of npx skills add — edit source and reinstall; never npx skills remove (local source path deletes the source itself)
Staffing provider trees under .agents/ are compiled by staffing's apply step — regenerate, don't hand-edit
Commits: imperative, sentence-case subject, no Conventional Commits prefix; Claude Code Co-Authored-By trailer; commit/push only when Asher asks

This repo is the no-running-app case: honest proof is the probe-eval transcript (plus rendered-HTML screenshots for skills that emit one); on a repo with an app, probe transcripts are NOT a substitute
Research dossiers stay under research/ (or <skill>-workspace/research/) — never copied into evidence/; citations are intrinsic provenance

Per change type: skill behavior → probe-eval transcript + pass/fail table, with before/after verdict shift for reworks; script → py_compile transcript + driven path (e.g. review-server.py --sweep output); visual artifact → before/after screenshots or short GIF; pure prose → nothing beyond the diff
A green 'it renders' screenshot alone is not enough when the change is about what the skill decides
Styling-only captures reusable only when HEAD is final reviewed HEAD and Reviewer records 'no product-code change; no recapture'
AFK run owes the full evidence package; interactively witnessed work may degrade to PR-body verification grades

GIF recipe: ffmpeg -i in.mp4 -filter_complex "fps=12,scale=960:-1:flags=lanczos,split[a][b];[a]palettegen[p];[b][p]paletteuse" out.gif; keep under 10 MB (GitHub inline ceiling); commit PNG/JPEG/GIF under evidence/<slug>/, never MP4; name c<criterion>-<what-it-shows>.png
GitHub embeds: same-origin blob URLs with ?raw=1 render on private repos; raw.githubusercontent.com and /raw/<sha>/ are camo-proxied and 404 on private repos; no API/CLI path to the drag-and-drop CDN
Embed form: [![<criterion>](https://github.com/<o>/<r>/blob/<sha>/evidence/<slug>/<f>.png?raw=1)](same URL without ?raw=1)

Verify mechanically, not by eye: gh api repos/<o>/<r>/commits/<sha> (404 = unpushed/orphaned); git cat-file -e <sha>:evidence/<slug>/<file>; extension PNG/JPEG/GIF never MP4; broken embed is almost always an orphaned SHA — re-pin, don't switch to plain links
Local binding: repo-relative embeds ../../evidence/<slug>/<file>.png; publish the review file WITH its evidence/ tree (directory root, not lone file) or skip the publish

No unit-test framework: 'tests' are probe scenarios driven through an executor; test-first here = write/adjust the probe scenario and answer key first, confirm the current skill fails it, then change and re-run
Tests live in each skill's own evals/ directory (skills are self-contained)
Commit style: imperative sentence-case subject, no Conventional Commits prefix; Claude Code Co-Authored-By trailer; commit/push only when Asher asks

UI: load bare-minimum-ux sibling where installed (wins on conflict); when external-dependencies.lock.json records consented impeccable, PRODUCT.md/DESIGN.md are ambient context (DESIGN.md wins visual, PRODUCT.md strategy/voice)
Repo conventions reviewers expect: self-contained skill dirs, compose-by-name, stdlib-only Python 3, passing probe eval for new/reworked skills
Two tiers: Tier 1 situated probes (comprehension), Tier 2 scripted dry-run (tool mechanics, no model in loop)
Method validated 2026-07-04 (triage skill, 20/20); reused for bayes (20/20 before ship) and maquette
Situated probes not quiz questions: 'you are at step X, criterion 3 fails — next concrete action?'

Context fidelity: separate sessions per deployment context — command-surface (SKILL.md in context) vs dispatched-thread (dispatch prompt only, no SKILL.md) — tests that disclosed references stand alone
Require citations: 'cite the file and exact sentence that decided it'
Answer key written BEFORE any runs; writing it after seeing output invalidates the eval
Executors are the deployment targets: from Claude Code, in-session Claude subagent + codex exec --sandbox read-only; from Codex, claude -p (never --bare) + native Codex agent
Ambiguity is a valid answer — flagged ambiguities are routinely the most valuable findings
Canonical Tier 1 example: skills/creative/maquette/evals/probes.md; canonical Tier 2: skills/personal/fair-deal/eval/protocol-dryrun.sh (35 checks, mktemp sandbox, bare local repo as remote)
Ship eval assets in the skill's own evals/ so reworks re-run them
Probe the failure branches and resumes, not just the happy path
Instances: triage (origin), bayes, maquette (Tier 1); fair-deal (Tier 2); shadixfy (evals/evals.json harness); skill-loop consumes these as eval input
Checks narrowest-first: probe eval per probe-evals.md; script check python3 -m py_compile then drive directly (confirm runs on this machine's Python 3.14); no npm/lint/typecheck/build pipeline exists
Site drift check: any change touching an SDLC-family skill runs python3 site/check.py (manifest must list the family's real files per site/MAINTENANCE.md); errors block
Aggregate gate before PR: changed skill's probe eval passes against answer key + changed scripts compile and run; new/reworked skills clear a pre-deployment probe eval before first real use
Second-opinion: delegate a scenario to codex exec as a differently-modeled executor for subjective criteria; grading and script-running stay local

Cold-reader check: a subagent with none of the authoring conversation reads the artifact alone and flags every ungroundable sentence; authoring-context leakage ('now', 'no longer', 'replaces', collaborator-addressed justifications) fixed before review-ready
CI merge gate: none — merge readiness is green probe eval + review LGTM, not a status check

Acceptance criteria by entryway: ticketed run reads ticket's acceptance block; spec without tickets reads the spec; interactive reads loop-recorded criteria in the issue thread; a skill's answer key is the executable form of its criteria
Every change must satisfy: file-level self-containment, compose-by-name, stdlib-only Python 3 scripts, probe eval for new/reworked skills, agents/openai.yaml where a skill must present well in Codex
Rendered HTML artifacts exercised in happy/empty/error states and both color schemes; bare-minimum-ux accessibility basics where installed

Repo-specific review concerns: self-containment (no cross-skill file imports), stdlib-only Python 3 scripts, agents/openai.yaml presence where Codex presentation matters, passing probe eval for new/reworked skills; Reviewer also judges rendered plan/prototype HTML for taste
Authoring-context leakage in shipped audience-facing text is a BLOCKER: quote the sentence, name the real recipient, require deletion or recipient-designed rewrite
Open every cited test/fixture/probe and confirm it exercises the claimed runtime seam before accepting it
Product-semantics questions are ruling requests to the issue coordinator, not review edits — wait for explicit ruling, then review the new HEAD
Approval = exact 'LGTM' comment when no actionable improvement remains
Styling-only evidence reuse verdict must be exactly 'no product-code change; no recapture' and only when HEAD is still final
~1000-line file growth is a presumptive blocker (shipped default, retained here)

Reviewer's structural pushes are in scope by default; reply per comment with commit SHA; intentional non-fixes reply with reason and risk; re-prompt the Reviewer once the iteration is handled (all shipped-default text, no repo-specific additions)
No PR template — use the outline; title imperative sentence-case matching commit style
Include 'Closes #<n>' so the issue closes on merge; under Claude Code the body ends with the '🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)' line
CI status line states plainly: no CI — readiness is green probe eval + LGTM
For research work-type, replace Evidence with 'Research dossier': link canonical dossier, state as-of boundary and claim-audit result, no duplicate evidence package
Evidence section is normally a placeholder: 'Captured after review converges.'

No unit-test framework: a skill bug is a wrong decision, broken route, or failing script; reproduce with the triggering situated probe through BOTH executors; the reproducing scenario becomes a probe in that skill's evals/
Script bugs: python3 -m py_compile then drive the failing path
Known flaky surface: model-dependent skill behavior — reproduce across at least two executor runs (Claude + codex exec) and rerun the same corpus before calling it fixed
Probe-eval equivalence is the regression seam for prose skills
No task runner/build system; prototypes are standalone HTML, scratch scenario runs, or stdlib-Python spikes
Rendered HTML prototypes must be self-contained single files (inline CSS/JS) so they render on the tailnet surface with no external assets
Keep prototypes OUTSIDE any skill directory — under the relevant <skill>-workspace/ or the session scratchpad — throwaway code never ships inside a self-contained skill

The behavior lock is a probe eval: capture affected skill's probe verdicts before the change; behavior-preserving only if identical scenarios produce the same verdicts after, re-run >=2 executors for model variance
Script refactors: py_compile plus the same driven paths producing the same output
A refactor must not turn a compose-by-name pointer into a file dependency; honors the copy-a-technique/extract-a-primitive rule
Durable root research/<slug>/; skill-specific investigations may live in the relevant <skill>-workspace/
Scratch shards go to the system temp dir, not committed; research stays out of evidence/ unless it proves a separate change criterion
Primary local sources: skill sources, git history, eval artifacts, project playbooks; cite stable file/line, commit, URL, page, or record locators; never publish credentials
Serialize writes to shared research artifacts; present durable findings as repo-local Markdown/HTML per the global Presentation policy

hazards: NEVER run npx skills remove here: skills-lock.json records a local source path, so remove deletes the skill SOURCE under skills/, not just the mount (documented in AGENTS.md and codebase.md). A naive CLI-driven cleanup destroys the canonical v2 sources.
Reinstall must be one atomic npx skills add with the COMPLETE desired skill set — sequential single-skill adds replace earlier selections (AGENTS.md § Source & updates).

writing-great-skills has no source under skills/ — the git-tracked mount .agents/skills/writing-great-skills is its only working-tree copy; a mount wipe + lockfile-driven reinstall cannot restore it (recoverable only from git history / upstream mattpocock/skills).

The v1 backlog mount holds files absent from the v2 source (scripts/run-state.py, evals/test_run_state.py, evals/liveness-probes.md, reference/, several templates incl. software/change-fixer.md, change-reviewer.md, implementing.md, refactoring.md, verifying.md); deleting the mount loses the working-tree copy of the v1 machinery (git history retains it).

docs/agents/*.md are repo-owned and dense with earned, live-verified content (gh dependency-API write commands, tailnet serve/reap recipes, catalog/site gate commands, the staffing byte budget, the zsh == trap, evidence embed mechanics). 'Delete old playbooks and re-scaffold' loses all of it — setups must reconcile, never overwrite.

Staffing has a declared provider-variant contract in .agents/asher-skills/variant-lock.json, but .claude/skills/staffing is currently a symlink to the codex mount — a naive reinstall could either clobber the variant contract or silently perpetuate the invalid symlink state; the migration must resolve which is intended.

The migration branch itself (sdlc-lifecycle, PR #90) is checked out in the main working copy; anything that resets/cleans the checkout risks the in-flight v2 work. Unmerged branches 80-decouple-planning-from-run, 84-interview-domain-modeling, codex/research-skill carry unmerged commits; the reconcile-consumers worktree is on a DETACHED HEAD.
Issue #80 is ready-for-agent — an old-family backlog run started mid-migration would dispatch it under v1 semantics.

~/.backlog/ (runs, state, reviews, review-state, surface, handoffs) is shared across projects (metis, pipelines, integrations-v2) — cleanup of 'backlog state' must be scoped to asher-skills entries only, and the live tailscale serve handlers (/asher-skills, /asher-skills/44/review, /asher-skills/until-zero/review, stale /review:8377) reference it.

backlog-policy.md's identity binding for needs-shaping does not match the live tracker (label is needs-spec) — a migration that trusts the playbook verbatim will bind a nonexistent label; one that re-runs setup blindly may mint a duplicate label instead of reusing needs-spec.
Mounts are git-tracked: any mount replacement is a working-tree change on the currently checked-out migration branch — commit hygiene required so v1 mount deletion is reviewable and reversible.

earned_content: Branch naming: codex/<issue-number>-<short-slug>; base main; PR target main
CI: GitHub Actions runs pnpm install --frozen-lockfile + pnpm check, but main has NO branch protection — checks do not block merge at platform layer
Preview deploys: pnpm --filter @workspace/backend deploy:vercel creates per-branch Convex preview, seeded by preview_seed:run
Pinned pnpm@10.4.1 — a mismatched major pnpm can try to purge node_modules (nested scripts call pnpm too)
Bootstrap a new worktree from inside it: node scripts/setup-worktree.mjs (copies missing env files, writes overlay, pnpm install)
pnpm dev:isolated = per-worktree ports/routes/local Convex + auto local_seed:run; pnpm dev:seed:isolated repeats seed once; pnpm worktree:env regenerates overlay
Cold-start caveat: first pnpm dev:isolated may exceed its fixed 30s readiness window while per-worktree Convex binary/dashboard downloads; rerun after caches warm
dotenvx run -- <command> for anything needing root .env values
Services: apps/web via portless app.onboardings next dev --turbopack; packages/backend convex dev; packages/emails portless email.onboardings; packages/ui Ladle at ui.onboardings
Local hostnames: app.onboardings.localhost, email.onboardings.localhost, ui.onboardings.localhost

Isolation regime local-isolatable; generated state: .env.worktree, packages/backend/.env.worktree, .worktree/convex-home (worktree HOME so anonymous Convex deployments don't share SQLite), .worktree/portless

Shared-singleton audit table (effect-verified 2026-07-14 with two simultaneous worktrees): Convex/ports/env isolatable; Clerk dev instance NOT (serialize auth mutation, suffix-namespaced orgs); AgentMail inbox NOT (serialize OTP, match fresh message); dev:reset targets NOT (exclusive human-approved lane)
Serialized exception lane: ALL isolated stack startups/seeds serialize (dev:isolated auto-seeds Clerk); after ready+authenticated, non-auth work runs concurrently

Seeds: pnpm dev:seed (dry-run), pnpm dev:seed -- --apply, pnpm dev:reset-and-seed, pnpm dev:reset -- --apply; reset DELETES all Clerk orgs in the test instance + app-owned Convex dev rows (after a Convex backup) — never while others use the shared stack
Seed contents: Dunn Harland, Demo Dealer, Demo System Owner orgs; Asher and AgentMail users; Demo System; one project per workflow stage (12 projects)
Isolated seed refuses live Clerk keys, never deletes Clerk data, creates orgs named 'Dunn Harland [<suffix>]'; NO verified safe command exists to remove these smoke orgs — leave them
Auth: Clerk JWTs trusted by Convex; public routes /sign-in and /invitations only

Agent auth path: seeded onboardings-dev@agentmail.to mailbox, request fresh OTP, consume programmatically without printing it; do NOT trust the initially-active org — smoke inherited the other worktree's active organization before switching
Fixture naming: VERIFY-<issue>-<purpose> for app records, [<worktree-suffix>] for shared-Clerk orgs
Never run shared reset commands as cleanup; never share a mutable scratch entity across issues
Browser: agent-browser, one isolated named session per worktree; ChatGPT-in-Chrome only on explicit user request; Computer Use disabled by default, per-task authorization only, never carries forward
Evidence formats: PNG/JPEG static, short sanitized GIF for flows; delete the full authentication recording; review captures for secrets

Review surface (v1 review-loop binding): static server rooted at ~/.backlog/surface via LaunchAgent com.asher.backlog-surface port 8377 keep-alive, proxied via tailscale serve --bg --set-path /review http://localhost:8377; root URL https://ashers-macbook-pro.tail045dd5.ts.net/review; Funnel stays off
Publish static doc: ln -sfn <file> ~/.backlog/surface/integrations-v2/<issue>/<name>.html

Interactive review: python3 .agents/skills/review-loop/scripts/review-server.py --doc ... --state ~/.backlog/review-state/integrations-v2/<issue>-<artifact> ... then tailscale serve --bg --set-path /review/integrations-v2/<issue>/review http://localhost:<port>
Verdict await: review-await.py exit codes — 0 approve, 3 approve-with-nits, 10 request-changes, 124 timeout
Stop/sweep: review-server.py --stop / --sweep; then tailscale serve --set-path ... off; tailscale serve status lists live handlers
Connectivity preflight: tailscale status before publishing; tailscale up only when node down and review publishing now; never Funnel

Parallelism verdict: serialize-verification (Clerk + AgentMail shared singletons); orchestrator owns the serialized lane and must not overlap it across issue threads; build/static-analysis/isolated non-auth tests may parallelize
Known environment gaps: cold Convex download timeout; no targeted Clerk-org cleanup
Tracker binding: github dunnharland/integrations-v2
gh issue view, gh pr view, gh pr edit ALL FAIL on this repo (exit 1, deprecated Projects-classic GraphQL; verified 2026-07-11) — use gh api REST verbs instead; gh pr list and gh pr checks work
Read issue: gh api repos/dunnharland/integrations-v2/issues/<n> (+ /comments)

Blocker read: gh api repos/dunnharland/integrations-v2/issues/<blocked>/dependencies/blocked_by (exercised end-to-end 2026-07-14); write: resolve numeric id via --jq '.id' then POST/DELETE -F issue_id=<id> to the same endpoint
Edit PR body: gh api --method PATCH repos/dunnharland/integrations-v2/pulls/<n> --input - with {"body": ...}
Close-on-merge: Closes #<n> in PR body
Merge policy: human squash-merges (gh pr merge <n> --squash) — house policy, NOT platform-enforced; --delete-branch fails on local branches while worktree exists — remove worktrees first

Stacked-PR reconciliation after base squash-merge: merge origin/main into stacked branch, conflicts are one-sided (keep both additions), run pnpm install + pnpm check before pushing; stacked PR merges strictly after base and PR body must name the order
Worktree create: git worktree add <path> -b codex/<issue>-<slug> origin/main

Harness binding: Codex native agent threads; cross-harness only via a named native wrapper subagent (e.g. external_claude_<role>) staffed with the cheapest eligible native model — babysit/relay only, no judgment; never raw fire-and-forget background shell
SHA-pinned semantics: rebase/force-push orphans pinned commits — re-pin branch-head SHA after any history rewrite

Label bindings: ready-for-agent, in-flight (claim marker set by run, branch+date recorded in a comment since GitHub has no native claim field), ready-for-human (also the abort target for verify caps and environment blockers), needs-info, needs-triage (neutral, not runnable)
in-review and closed need no labels — expressed natively by an open PR and issue closure

Work-type labels: bug → diagnose branch; enhancement → plan→implement; refactor; research (dossier under research/<slug>/); draft → judgment-terminal produce-and-review, done = human review verdict, artifact kept (unlike prototype)
Tie-break rule: sources-terminal → research; prose judged by voice/fit → draft; behavior changes keep code work-type with research as substage

Dispatch metadata block required in issue body before ready-for-agent: Surface (backend/ui/mixed/non-code), Required capabilities, Coordination (routine | orchestrator-required), Reason, Known uncertainty; missing metadata → run skips, never infers
Exclusions: duplicate/invalid/wontfix; 'superseded' role maps to wontfix + linking comment (no separate label exists)
Neutral labels: codex, design, documentation, good first issue, help wanted, question
Dependencies use GitHub native blocked_by relation (verbs in platform.md); run skips while any blocker open

In-flight hygiene: optimistic claim, run re-reads each issue immediately before marking; orphan = recorded branch gone or quiet past 7 days → groom surfaces to human as candidate reset, NEVER silently reset (branch may hold unmerged work)
Prefer types over interfaces; strippable TS only (no enums); avoid as-casts unless commented; always T[] / readonly T[]
Always use attempt/attemptAsync from es-toolkit instead of try-catch
Early returns over nesting; kebab-case filenames; no non-ASCII punctuation (en dash, curly apostrophe) in output
No barrel files (index.ts re-exports) anywhere — import directly from the source file
next-intl WITHOUT locale URL routing; locale resolved per request in apps/web/i18n/request.ts: locale cookie → Accept-Language → English
Supported locales/default/direction in packages/backend/convex/locales.ts (shared web+Convex); native names + cookie name in apps/web/i18n/config.ts

User language stored in userPreferences Convex table, mirrored to cookie by apps/web/components/locale-sync.tsx; selector apps/web/components/language-select.tsx; Clerk localized via @clerk/localizations in apps/web/i18n/clerk-localization.ts
Catalogs at apps/web/messages/<locale>.json; en.json canonical; non-English deep-merged over English at request time so passes can land one namespace at a time
Namespace per screen/component cluster; no grab-bag common namespace; camelCase keys; ICU syntax; identical placeholders across locales
apps/web/i18n/messages.test.ts enforces catalog-per-locale, subset keys, no empty values — run with pnpm --filter web test
packages/ui stays locale-agnostic — text via props, no i18n provider in packages/ui
Never hardcode locale in Intl.*Format; useLocale() client / getLocale() server

Enumerated remaining hardcoded en-US call sites: invitation-gantt.tsx, migration-form.tsx, project-tasks-workspace.tsx, project-user-stories-workspace.tsx, data-validation-panel.tsx, generate-config-document.ts, schedule-options.tsx (locale prop), packages/backend/convex/projects.ts (en-CA, needs recipient's stored preference)

RTL: getTextDirection(locale) sets <html dir>; Radix DirectionProvider wraps tree; use logical Tailwind utilities (ms-*, me-*, ps-*, pe-*, start-*, end-*) in i18n-touched code; sidebar mirrors via side prop from orgs/[slug]/layout.tsx
Adding a locale: SUPPORTED_LOCALES (+RTL_LOCALES) in convex/locales.ts → LOCALE_NATIVE_NAMES in config.ts → clerk-localization mapping → messages/<locale>.json (partial ok)

Translation-pass backlog: platform/settings, project-wizard, project-tasks, project-user-stories, migration-config, validation, specification-config, project-versions still hardcoded English; verify in one translated locale AND Arabic (RTL) before merging; emails not yet localized — recipient locale should come from userPreferences
Targeted tests: pnpm --filter web test <path> and pnpm --filter @workspace/backend test <path> — do NOT insert '--': it runs the full suite (web 99 files/615 tests, backend 55 files/478 tests)

Package checks: pnpm --filter web check / @workspace/backend check / @workspace/ui check / @workspace/emails check; pnpm lint (green, 15 warnings), pnpm typecheck, pnpm format, pnpm build, pnpm lint:ws, aggregate pnpm check
Recorded red baseline (as of 2026-07-14): web check / typecheck / build / pnpm check red on TS2307 missing 'mermaid' module from apps/web/lib/block-document-export/mermaid-image.ts
CI: .github/workflows/check.yml runs same pnpm check; no required status checks, main not branch-protected; house policy = disclose CI status and get green check before human merge
Each claimed criterion names the actual runtime seam; open cited tests/fixtures and confirm they cover it
Auth/org/role/invitation/email/data-export surfaces need explicit permission or side-effect criteria

Evidence bar per change type: logic/backend fix = green targeted test + pnpm check; UI = screenshot/browser before-after; workflow/auth = app-level walkthrough with account/org, expected, actual; email = rendered/captured preview without secrets; seed/reset/deploy = dry-run or safe-env proof with destructive commands called out
Targeted test invocation repeated: package-relative path, NO '--' separator (adding -- runs the full package suite)
Tests live: web under apps/web; backend under packages/backend/convex and packages/backend/mcp; Vitest via Turbo/pnpm filters
Commit style: concise imperative subject; Conventional Commits NOT enforced
Never commit .env values, generated backups, .next, .turbo, caches, node_modules
pnpm/pnpm dlx only; dotenvx run -- for root .env commands
Convex filenames under packages/backend/convex: only alphanumerics, underscores, periods
Use nuqs when React screen state should be URL-shareable
Prefer autosave forms and optimistic Convex updates over explicit save buttons
Under-specified issue → stop and move back to needs-info instead of guessing

Plan threshold: multi-subsystem (web UI + Convex), data models/authz/migrations/public API, seeded data/auth flows/email/preview-deploy behavior, high-risk workflow state/permissions/invitations/generated artifacts, or >~1 focused day
Plan location: .plans/<issue-number>-<short-slug>.html — self-contained HTML, inline SVG diagrams pre-rendered (mmdc), stable anchors, acceptance criteria as <li id="ac-N" data-criterion>
Approval happens on the review surface (review-loop), hash-bound to the version seen; request-changes requires a ledger disposition per annotation (changed/kept/orphaned) before re-awaiting
Approved plan is committed to the work branch BEFORE implementation dispatch; issue gets a markdown digest comment + repo link to the committed HTML
Evidence must be real app-driven artifacts; probe/dry-run transcripts are not evidence in this repo
Artifacts committed under evidence/<slug>/, named c<criterion>-<what-it-shows>.png; never commit MP4 (GitHub can't render committed video)
GIF recipe: ffmpeg two-pass palette (fps=12,scale=960, palettegen/paletteuse), ≤~10s, well under 10 MB

GitHub embed form: [![crit](https://github.com/<o>/<r>/blob/<sha>/evidence/<slug>/<f>.png?raw=1)](...plain blob url...) — blob+?raw=1 is same-origin and works on private repos; raw.githubusercontent.com and /raw/<sha>/ are camo-proxied and 404 on private repos
Mechanical verification (agent can't view renders): gh api commits/<sha> proves SHA pushed; git cat-file -e <sha>:evidence/<slug>/<file> proves path; extension PNG/JPEG/GIF only
Broken embed almost always = orphaned SHA after rewrite — re-pin, never switch to plain links
Styling-only evidence reuse allowed only when HEAD is the final reviewed HEAD and Reviewer records verbatim 'no product-code change; no recapture'

PR body order: Closes #<n> + work-type, Summary (incl. scope discovery), Changes (design reasoning not file list), Plan (SHA-pinned), Checks run, CI status (disclose red/pending AND lack of branch protection — never describe workflow as platform-enforced), Verification (per-criterion, workarounds framed as environment gaps vs product issues), Evidence placeholder 'Captured after review converges'
research work-type: Research dossier section instead of Evidence — link canonical dossier, as-of boundary, claim-audit result
gh pr edit fails on this repo — PATCH via gh api repos/<o>/<r>/pulls/<n> --input -

Repo-specific scrutiny list: authorization and tenant/org isolation (Clerk claims + Convex access checks); data model/schema/validator/generated-API compatibility; collaboration and optimistic-update behavior; email/invitation/webhook/external side effects; frontend a11y/responsive/no overlapping text/design-system conventions/shareable URL state; direct imports, no barrel files, pnpm-only, Convex filename rules, no secret leakage
~1000-line file growth is a presumptive blocker — ask for decomposition first
Product-semantics question = ruling request to the issue coordinator, never a review edit; after Fixer applies ruling, Reviewer re-reviews new HEAD
Approval = exact 'LGTM' comment; styling-evidence reuse verdict must be verbatim 'no product-code change; no recapture' and only when HEAD still final
Run targeted checks then pnpm check for code changes; disclose the recorded pre-existing baseline rather than claiming green; Markdown-only fixes need no pnpm check unless the PR already changed code
Reviewer evaluates the pushed HEAD — commit and push before handing back; reply per comment with pushed SHA; escalate product-semantics rulings to the issue coordinator
Vitest; web tests **/*.test.ts under apps/web (Node env); backend under packages/backend/convex and packages/backend/mcp; targeted run without '--' (with -- runs full suite)

App-visible bugs: pnpm dev:isolated (auto-seeds worktree-local Convex + suffix-namespaced shared-Clerk orgs), serialize startup/auth, drive printed worktree URL with isolated named agent-browser session; record exact URL, account/org, seed namespace, observed behavior

Investigation layers for Convex-backed paths: web caller → generated API usage → validator/schema → mutation/query/action logic → Clerk/Convex auth state; include packages/backend/mcp when failure crosses the MCP surface
No repo logging/debugger/profiler entry points recorded; no flaky tests recorded; shared Clerk tenant remains the collision hazard
Done gate: targeted checks + pnpm check green, or isolate and disclose the recorded baseline failure
Task runner: pnpm scripts + Turbo, register in owning package's package.json (web: apps/web)
Routing: Next.js App Router under apps/web; shared UI in packages/ui with Ladle preview at ui.onboardings.localhost
UI variants must use @workspace/ui components and existing design-system conventions; screenshot variants via the agent-browser driver and embed in the plan with winner marked
Durable dossier root: research/<slug>/; scratch shards in system temp, uncommitted; research stays out of evidence/ unless it proves a change criterion
Primary external sources: official integration-provider documentation and APIs, via authenticated connectors or browser sessions
Serialize rate-limited or tenant-mutating sources; preserve tenant data and credentials
Lock behavior at public seams first; characterization tests per implementing.md standards; if no testable seam, creating one is the first separate minimal refactor
Preserve public behavior, URL state, authz behavior, generated API contracts, and seed/reset behavior; Convex filename rules and generated-API compatibility; no barrel files
Never change behavior and structure in the same commit; a locked test that must change = behavior change → stop for a product ruling
Same targeted-test and package-check commands as verifying.md; pnpm check before PR unless documented baseline

earned_content: Label bindings 1:1, no aliases: ready-for-agent, in-flight, ready-for-human, needs-info (readiness); bug, enhancement, refactor, research, draft (work type); duplicate/invalid/wontfix/superseded (exclusion); documentation/good first issue/help wanted/question/needs-triage/design/observability/rfq/admin are neutral
in-review and closed are expressed natively on GitHub (open PR / native closure) — no labels for them
ready-for-agent must never be present without exactly one of bug/enhancement/refactor/research/draft
in-flight is set by run at dispatch replacing ready-for-agent; branch name + dispatch date recorded in a comment alongside the label
ready-for-human doubles as the abort target for verify caps and environment blockers, with the blocker commented
draft = judgment-terminal produce-and-review work; done = human review verdict, artifact is kept (unlike prototype); research = epistemic-terminal, dossier under research/<slug>/

Dispatch metadata (Surface: backend/ui/mixed/non-code; Coordination class: routine vs orchestrator-required; reason) stored in a stable 'Dispatch:' block in issue body/grooming comment; missing metadata = run skips the issue

Dependencies: read blockers via gh api -H "X-GitHub-Api-Version: 2026-03-10" repos/dunnharland/pipelines/issues/<n>/dependencies/blocked_by; write by resolving blocker numeric id (gh api ... --jq '.id') then POST {"issue_id": <id>}
GOTCHA (2026-07-14 live probe): .issue_dependencies_summary stayed zero while the list endpoint worked — never use the summary as the run-queue read path
Orphan sweep: in-flight issue whose recorded branch is gone or quiet >7 days is a corpse; surface to human for reset, never silently reset (branch may hold unmerged work)
Keep Syncron inbound batch behavior explicit in issues: ingress path, content date handling, required files, transformation feature state, egress destination, acceptance evidence
UI-heavy work requires docs/agents/frontend.md plus the local UI skills before implementation
DB enum/domain-state changes require a type-safety acceptance criterion: bun run check must fail stale callers across database, API, SQL, and frontend surfaces

Base branch main; branch naming codex/<issue-number>-<slug> (codex/<short-slug> without an issue); PR target main; CI = bun install --frozen-lockfile + bun run check; no per-PR preview deploys; Railway deploy documented in docs/deploy/railway.md — confirm before assuming auto-deploy
bun install; bun run dev starts Postgres, MinIO, upload-bucket initializer, local SFTP egress, shared portless proxy, and Turbo dev tasks for backend/frontend/sftp-ingress/worker/status page
URLs: http://frontend.pipelines.localhost:1355, http://backend.pipelines.localhost:1355, http://status.pipelines.localhost:1355
Stack commands: bun run db:up, bucket:up, sftp-egress:up, reset:db, reset:object-storage, reset:sftp; migration: bun run db:migrate

Worktree isolation via scripts/worktree-env.ts, with-worktree-env.ts, setup-worktree.sh, reap-worktree-stacks.ts: derives COMPOSE_PROJECT_NAME=pipelines-<suffix>, unique ports 21000-31000 (Postgres/MinIO/SFTP), per-worktree *.localhost:1355 hostnames, per-worktree DATABASE_URL/S3_ENDPOINT/auth origins/webhook URLs
Cleanup: scripts/cleanup-worktree.sh; bun run reset:worktree-stacks for orphans; bun scripts/reap-worktree-stacks.ts --all; escape hatch PIPELINES_WORKTREE_ISOLATION=off

portless proxy port 1355 is intentionally shared across worktrees (per-worktree subdomains); Railway, Resend, AgentMail, OpenAI, PostHog, Sentry, customer SFTP are NOT isolatable — serialize or use noop/mocks

Seed: explicit QA fixtures only, app never seeds; bun run qa:seed-rfq-comparison (uses QA_ORG_SLUG + AGENT_EMAIL, refuses non-local DB hosts, worktree-aware, applies pending migrations); fixtures via bun run qa:generate-syncron-inbound-batch -- --content-date today; journeys in docs/qa/journeys/
Auth: Better Auth email OTP; local AUTH_EMAIL_PROVIDER=noop logs OTP/invites in backend console; PLATFORM_ADMIN_EMAILS controls platform admin; agentmail when AGENTMAIL_API_KEY + AGENT_EMAIL set

Browser policy: agent-browser with one isolated named session per check is primary (plan asher-skills#73); ChatGPT-in-Chrome is a per-use-consent carve-out only; repo-owned Puppeteer (bun run qa:browser:probe) is the authorized fallback (2026-07-22), owns bundled Chrome, never attaches to user profile

Web baseline: bun run dev, wait for HTTP 200 on frontend + backend /api/health + status page, then drive frontend via agent-browser; missing AGENT_EMAIL or QA_ORG_SLUG is an explicit blocker, not permission to invent an account
Load fixtures (qa:generate-syncron-inbound-load-batch) only for scale/memory criteria; fixture names include issue number + timestamp; owning issue thread cleans up with reset:* commands

Review surface: static root https://ashers-macbook-pro.tail045dd5.ts.net/review backed by ~/.backlog/surface (LaunchAgent com.asher.backlog-surface, port 8377); repo hub ~/.backlog/surface/pipelines; publish via ln -sfn <file> ~/.backlog/surface/pipelines/<scope>/<name>.html; Funnel stays off; run tailscale status before publishing

Interactive review: python3 .agents/skills/review-loop/scripts/review-server.py --doc ... --surface ~/.backlog/surface/pipelines --public-url https://ashers-macbook-pro.tail045dd5.ts.net/pipelines/<scope>/review ...; proxy via tailscale serve --bg --set-path /pipelines/<scope>/review http://localhost:<port>; await via review-await.py (exit 0 approve, 3 approve-with-nits, 10 request-changes, 124 timeout); teardown --stop then tailscale serve --set-path ... off; sweep via --sweep

Staffing evidence (2026-07-14): codex-cli 0.144.3 reaches gpt-5.6-sol; claude 2.1.207 sonnet/opus aliases effect-verified; versioned aliases fable-5/sonnet-5/opus-4.8 REJECTED by installed Claude CLI; Claude→codex exec unavailable in unattended children — fall back within harness

Parallelism verdict: parallel-safe (isolation has regression tests — 15 checks in scripts/worktree-env.test.ts passed 2026-07-14); serialize anything touching shared Railway, real third-party providers, or external SFTP
Independent second-opinion verification: delegate to codex exec with a self-contained prompt

Tracker binding: github dunnharland/pipelines; verbs verified live at setup: gh issue list --state open --json number,title,labels; gh issue view <n> --comments; gh issue comment; gh issue edit --add/remove-label; gh issue create --label <work-type> (serialized main-branch writer only); gh issue close
Blocked-by read/write commands duplicated here with API version 2026-03-10 and the stale .issue_dependencies_summary warning
Close-on-merge: Closes #<n> in PR body

PR verbs: gh pr create --base main --body-file <file> (ready, not draft); gh pr edit --body-file; inline comments via gh api repos/dunnharland/pipelines/pulls/<n>/comments; approval = exact 'LGTM' comment; a human merges on GitHub — the loop never merges

Worktree create: git worktree add <path> -b codex/<issue-number>-<slug> origin/main; fetch+pull main before forking; publish git push -u origin <branch>; teardown git worktree remove then reap derived stack
Pinned-SHA semantics: rebase/force-push orphans pinned SHAs — re-pin after any history rewrite
Spawned threads can read backlog references at .agents/skills/backlog/ (symlinked as .claude/skills/backlog)
Claude Code spawns issue threads via Agent/Task tool with isolation: worktree; Codex via native collaboration agent threads
Evidence = real check output + artifacts driven through the actual app; probe/dry-run transcripts are NOT evidence where a runnable surface exists

Captured once after adversarial review converges, mapped per acceptance criterion; styling-only captures reusable only when HEAD is final reviewed HEAD and Reviewer records exactly 'no product-code change; no recapture'
Commit PNG/JPEG/GIF under evidence/<slug>/, never MP4 (GitHub can't render committed video); name c<criterion>-<what-it-shows>.png
GIF recipe: ffmpeg -i in.mp4 -filter_complex "fps=12,scale=960:-1:flags=lanczos,split[a][b];[a]palettegen[p];[b][p]paletteuse" out.gif, well under 10 MB

GitHub embed form: [![<criterion>](https://github.com/<owner>/<repo>/blob/<sha>/evidence/<slug>/<file>.png?raw=1)](...) — blob ?raw=1 URLs are same-origin and NOT camo-proxied so they render on private repos; raw.githubusercontent.com and /raw/<sha>/ ARE camo-proxied and 404 on private repos; no API path to the drag-and-drop CDN

Mechanical verification (agent can't view the render): check blob/<sha>/...?raw=1 syntax; SHA on remote via gh api repos/<o>/<r>/commits/<sha>; file exists via git cat-file -e <sha>:evidence/<slug>/<file>; extension PNG/JPEG/GIF
Broken embed is almost always an orphaned SHA — re-pin (or branch-name ref), never 'fix' by switching to plain links

PR body order: close linkage + work-type, Summary (incl. scope discovery), Changes (design reasoning, not file list), Plan (SHA-pinned link), Checks run, CI status, Verification (per-criterion with caveats), Evidence placeholder 'Captured after review converges'
research work uses 'Research dossier' section instead of Evidence: link canonical dossier, as-of boundary, claim-audit result
Title convention: sentence-case imperative summary; include issue number in PR title/body when available
Address every actionable comment unless the fix changes product behavior beyond issue scope; behavior-preserving structural improvements requested by Reviewer are in scope by default
Reply to each addressed comment with what changed and the commit SHA; intentional non-fix gets reason + remaining risk; re-prompt Reviewer once the iteration is handled
Run relevant checks from verifying.md after changes, then commit and push the iteration
Behavior-risk surfaces to scrutinize: Syncron inbound ingestion, validation, canonical identity, transform output, object storage, worker orchestration, SFTP egress
End-to-end type safety for persisted enum values, domain state strings, API contracts, SQL, and frontend call sites; migration safety, generated artifacts, rollback assumptions, stale-caller failures
Test coverage bar: regression tests for bugs, characterization for refactors, user-journey evidence for workflow changes
Frontend must follow docs/agents/frontend.md and handle loading/error/empty/disabled states
Security/ops: no secrets in logs, safe SFTP credentials, bounded database pools, no shared-resource assumptions hidden in tests
Product-semantics questions are ruling requests to the issue coordinator — wait for explicit ruling, don't invent behavior
Approval = exact 'LGTM' comment; styling-only evidence reuse verdict must be exactly 'no product-code change; no recapture' and only while HEAD is final
Tests live beside source as *.test.ts/*.test.tsx; run bun test <path> or bun run --cwd <package-or-app> test -- <path>
If a test is impractical, record exact command, fixture files, route, organization, content date, inputs
Syncron inbound bug triage: identify whether the failing path is manual upload, SFTP ingress, validation, canonical writes, transform, object storage, worker orchestration, or SFTP egress

Browser bugs routed to gpt-5.6-terra via chatgpt-in-chrome with agent-browser as backup — NOTE: conflicts with newer environment.md policy (agent-browser primary, 2026-07-22 Puppeteer fallback); reconcile during migration
Infra logs: bun run db:logs, bun run bucket:logs, bun run sftp-egress:logs
Worker/batch investigations must preserve the content-date filename path and daily-run assumptions; runner emits structured progress via injected logger wired in apps/worker/src/index.ts
Sentry init lives in packages/observability/src/server.ts; local observability sink at scripts/dev/observability-sink.ts
Known hard surface: worker event-loop freezes during analytics (issue #203) — start with apps/worker/src/freeze-forensics.test.ts, apps/worker/src/supervisor.test.ts, captured worker logs
Real customer SFTP endpoints and hosted Railway are shared — reproduce locally unless the issue explicitly authorizes the shared environment
Fire-and-forget mutations report via sonner toasts through apps/frontend/src/lib/feedback.ts (toastSuccess/toastError), optionally with action link to the affected resource
Form saves report inline next to the form's actions (account.profile.tsx pattern: success/error banner in form, SubmitButton blockedWhen for validation)

Query errors never swallowed: tables pass state/errorMessage/onRetry to DataTable; non-table surfaces branch on isError and render error-card + Retry (dashboard rail grammar); never render an empty/'No X yet' state from an errored or pending query
Settings forms seed drafts only from server data: skeleton while pending, error card with Retry, Save disabled until loaded
Use @workspace/ui components before adding primitives; keep operational screens dense and direct — no decorative copy, oversized panels, nested cards
Never <datalist> for app selectors — use Popover + Command or Combobox; custom entry stays inside the combobox flow with an explicit 'Use <value>' option

Add/insert pickers commit immediately on select (no separate Add click unless a real confirmation step exists); options contain only valid choices — filter already-selected/duplicate/unavailable/invalid instead of silent no-ops

Put add/insert controls where the item will appear (parent row/header/actions, not a placeholder row); render hierarchical data as collapsible tree, not a flat list; don't flatten composite selector values
Completion checks: grep touched selector UI for 'datalist' and 'list='; check pickers for select-then-confirm flows and invalid options; run frontend type-check and lint
Commit style: sentence-case imperative matching recent history, e.g. 'Fix RFQ comparison OOM: stop over-fetching item-supplier analytics'
Prefer simple in-memory processing for the small batch sizes this repo handles
Keep type sources narrow and exported: persisted enum values and domain state strings must not widen to string across package/API/SQL/frontend boundaries
Use the database-migrations playbook/skill before generating or editing migrations
For frontend work load uncodixfy, bare-minimum-ux, and docs/agents/frontend.md; add shadcn when touching shadcn/base-ui primitives
If implementation exposes a materially simpler/narrower path, stop and report before expanding scope; record plan deviations in the PR body with reason and verification impact

Plan threshold: >1 subsystem/public interface; DB schema, persisted enums, domain state strings, API contracts, file formats, SFTP behavior, or object storage layout; new dependency/external service/feature flag/background workflow; Syncron inbound run semantics, canonical identity, dedupe, egress delivery, manual upload; risky/hard-to-reverse/> ~1 day

Plans at plans/<YYYY-MM-DD-HHMM>-<slug>.html — self-contained HTML (inline styles/scripts, no external fetches), from the plan skill's templates/plan-skeleton.html; diagrams pre-rendered inline SVG (mmdc), never a shipped rendering library

Stable anchors: every section and reviewable item keeps a stable id across revisions; acceptance criteria are <li id="ac-N" data-criterion> rows; review annotations and verify/evidence key on those ids

Approval only via the review-loop surface's hash-bound approve event; a thread/tracker comment is NOT approval; on request-changes write a ledger disposition per annotation (changed/kept/orphaned) before re-awaiting
Local fallback: open the rendered HTML and take the verdict in conversation
Task runner: bun scripts + Turbo; register in the owning package's package.json; wrap database-backed scripts with bun scripts/with-worktree-env.ts
Frontend routing under apps/frontend/src; follow docs/agents/frontend.md
UI variants must use shadcn/base-ui primitives; load shadcn, uncodixfy, and bare-minimum-ux skills for UI work
UI prototypes host variants in the real page gated by ?variant= URL param with a floating switcher pill; screenshot each variant via the driver in environment.md
Lock behavior with characterization tests first; framework Bun test; bun test <path> or bun run --cwd <package-or-app> test -- <path>
Database-backed behavior runs against the isolated local stack through bun scripts/with-worktree-env.ts
Refactors must not widen persisted values and exported domain unions to raw string; avoid cross-package reshapes unless they remove real duplication
No behavior changes in refactor PRs — a real bug means stop and reclassify; final bun run check before PR handoff
Durable root research/<slug>/; scratch shards in system temp, not committed; research stays out of evidence/ unless it proves a change criterion
Primary external sources: official Syncron and provider documentation/APIs; authenticated connectors or browser sessions when required
Serialize shared Railway, SFTP, or third-party systems; preserve customer files, credentials, private endpoints
Check ladder: bun test <path>; bun scripts/with-worktree-env.ts ... for DB-backed; bun run --cwd <pkg> check; bun run format; bun run lint; bun run type-check; bun run build; full gate bun run check
bun run check runs sherif, knip --no-config-hints, and turbo run check --concurrency=1

CI merge gate: .github/workflows/ci.yml runs bun install --frozen-lockfile then bun run check — identical to the local full gate; PR not mergeable until green; local checks prove the change, CI-green is the merge condition
Setup audit (2026-07-14): format/lint/type-check/build/check all passed; known non-fatal frontend chunk-size advisory and Turbo warning that worker#build declares no output files
Second-opinion verification delegates to codex exec when a criterion needs real UI interaction/screenshots outside the current context
End-to-end type safety expectation: a stale caller should fail bun run check

Syncron inbound changes: verify ingress path, validation, canonical writes, transform output, object storage artifacts, SFTP egress; evidence = generated manifest path, run detail URL, final run status, egress artifact listing, row-level proof
Migrations: run against the isolated local stack before relying on app behavior; report migration command result + targeted proof, no secrets/.env values
UI evidence: browser-observed result or screenshot incl. loading/error/disabled states; SFTP evidence: local destination listing or transcript with credentials omitted
